values.content:
=<context>
<candidate_information>
Name: {{ $json.contact.name }}
Email: {{ $json.contact.email }}
Phone: {{ $json.contact.phone }}
Location: {{ $json.contact.location }}
LinkedIn: {{ $json.contact.linkedin }}
Portfolio: {{ $json.contact.portfolio }}
</candidate_information>
<resume_content>
<summary>
{{ $json.summary }}
</summary>
<experience>
{{ JSON.stringify($json.experience, null, 2) }}
</experience>
<education>
{{ JSON.stringify($json.education, null, 2) }}
</education>
<skills>
{{ JSON.stringify($json.skills, null, 2) }}
</skills>
<projects>
{{ JSON.stringify($json.projects, null, 2) }}
</projects>
<certifications>
{{ JSON.stringify($json.certifications, null, 2) }}
</certifications>
</resume_content>
<target_position>
Role: {{ $('Webhook1').item.json.body.job_role }}
Company: {{ $('Webhook1').item.json.body.company_name }}
</target_position>
</context>
<instructions>
Transform this structured data into professional markdown with these requirements:
<formatting_rules>

Use # for name (H1), ## for section headers (H2), ### for job titles (H3)
Use bullet points (- or *) for achievements and responsibilities
Bold (text) important keywords, metrics, and achievements
Keep formatting ATS-friendly - no tables, no complex layouts
Use consistent date formatting throughout
Organize sections logically and with proper spacing
Make content visually scannable with proper hierarchy
ONLY include sections that have actual data - skip empty sections
</formatting_rules>

<content_guidelines>

Highlight quantifiable achievements with bold
Ensure keywords from target role are visible and prominent
Use professional language and tone
Maintain clean, readable structure
Add appropriate spacing between sections
DO NOT add placeholder text for missing sections
USE ONLY the actual data provided above
</content_guidelines>
</instructions>

<output_format>
Return ONLY the markdown content. No explanations. No preamble. No code blocks.
Start directly with the markdown formatted resume using ONLY the actual data provided.
</output_format>
<data_usage_rules>
CRITICAL:

Use ONLY the data provided in the context above
DO NOT create example sections or placeholder content
If experience data is missing, skip the experience section
If skills are empty, skip the skills section
Format what exists, don't invent what doesn't
The name, email, and contact info MUST match exactly what was provided
</data_usage_rules>

<critical_reminder>

NO markdown code blocks in your response
NO "Here is the formatted resume:" or similar text
START immediately with the markdown content
Use clean, professional formatting
DO NOT MAKE UP ANY DATA - USE ONLY WHAT WAS PROVIDED
DO NOT ADD EXAMPLE TEXT OR PLACEHOLDERS
</critical_reminder>

=<role>
You are an expert resume formatter specializing in creating ATS-optimized, visually appealing resume layouts. You transform structured data into professional markdown that converts beautifully to PDF while maintaining ATS compatibility.
</role>
<expertise>

ATS-friendly formatting (no tables, no complex layouts)
Visual hierarchy and readability
Professional typography and spacing
Keyword placement and density optimization
Clean markdown syntax
</expertise>

<task>
Convert structured resume data into professional, clean markdown format that is both ATS-compatible and visually appealing when rendered.
</task>
<critical_rules>

Use ONLY standard markdown (headers, bold, bullets)
NO tables, NO columns, NO complex formatting
Bold important keywords and metrics
Maintain logical flow: Contact → Summary → Experience → Education → Skills → Projects → Certifications
Return ONLY markdown content - no explanations, no preambles
USE ONLY THE ACTUAL DATA PROVIDED - DO NOT MAKE UP ANY INFORMATION
DO NOT use placeholder text or example data
</critical_rules>

<anti_hallucination_rules>

DO NOT add sample text like "Lorem ipsum" or "Example text here"
DO NOT create placeholder sections if data is missing
DO NOT invent experience, skills, or projects not in the provided data
If a section has no data, skip it entirely
ONLY format the actual data provided in the input
</anti_hallucination_rules>

nodes.name: Gamma - Create Document1
nodes.notes: ⚠️ CONFIGURE YOUR GAMMA API ENDPOINT AND AUTH

parameters.subject: Your Optimized Resume is Ready! 🎯
parameters.text:
=Hi {{ $('Parse Analysis JSON1').item.json.improved_resume.contact.name }},

Great news! Your resume has been optimized for the {{ $('Webhook1').item.json.body.job_role }} position at {{ $('Webhook1').item.json.body.company_name }}

📊 Analysis Results:
- Alignment Score:{{ $('Parse Analysis JSON1').item.json.analysis.alignment_score }}%
- Keywords Matched: {{ $('Parse Analysis JSON1').item.json.analysis.matching_keywords.length }}
- Missing Keywords Added:{{ $('Parse Analysis JSON1').item.json.analysis.missing_keywords.length }}

✨ Your optimized resume is attached in both PDF format.

Key Improvements Made:
{{ $("Parse Analysis JSON1").item.json.improved_resume.improvements_made.join('\n• ') }}

Tips for Your Application:
1. Review the resume and customize it further if needed
2. Use keywords from the job description naturally in your cover letter
3. Tailor your LinkedIn profile to match your updated resume

Good luck with your application!

Best regards,
Resume Optimizer Team

values.content:
=<context>
<original_resume>
{{ $json.text }}
</original_resume>
<job_details>
<job_description>
{{ $('Webhook1').item.json.body.job_description }}
</job_description>
<target_role>{{ $('Webhook1').item.json.body.job_role }}</target_role>
<company>{{ $('Webhook1').item.json.body.company_name }}</company>
<industry>{{ $('Webhook1').item.json.body.industry }}</industry>
<experience_level>{{ $('Webhook1').item.json.body.experience_level }}</experience_level>
</job_details>
</context>
<instructions>
<step_1>
ANALYZE the resume against the job description:

Calculate alignment score (0-100) based on keyword matches and relevance
Identify all matching keywords between resume and job description
Identify critical missing keywords from job description
List the resume's strengths
Identify gaps and weaknesses
Provide specific, actionable recommendations
</step_1>

<step_2>
IMPROVE the resume using ONLY the candidate's actual information:

Extract ALL contact details EXACTLY as written in the original resume
Use the candidate's ACTUAL name, email, phone, location from the resume
Incorporate missing keywords naturally where they fit the candidate's EXISTING experience
Strengthen weak sections identified in gaps
Optimize for ATS scanning (proper formatting, keyword density)
Add metrics and quantifiable achievements where possible using ACTUAL data
DO NOT fabricate any information
Prioritize impact statements over responsibilities
Use strong action verbs
</step_2>
</instructions>

<output_format>
Return ONLY valid JSON with this EXACT structure (no markdown blocks, no code fences, no explanations):
{
"analysis": {
"alignment_score": 85,
"matching_keywords": ["actual keyword from resume", "another keyword"],
"missing_keywords": ["keyword from job description", "another missing keyword"],
"strengths": ["actual strength from resume", "another strength"],
"gaps": ["actual gap identified", "another gap"],
"recommendations": ["specific recommendation", "another recommendation"]
},
"improved_resume": {
"contact": {
"name": "ACTUAL NAME FROM RESUME - DO NOT MAKE UP",
"email": "ACTUAL EMAIL FROM RESUME - DO NOT MAKE UP",
"phone": "ACTUAL PHONE FROM RESUME OR EMPTY STRING",
"location": "ACTUAL LOCATION FROM RESUME OR EMPTY STRING",
"linkedin": "ACTUAL LINKEDIN FROM RESUME OR EMPTY STRING",
"portfolio": "ACTUAL PORTFOLIO FROM RESUME OR EMPTY STRING"
},
"summary": "2-3 powerful sentences optimized for target role using ACTUAL candidate background",
"experience": [
{
"title": "ACTUAL Job Title from resume",
"company": "ACTUAL Company Name from resume",
"duration": "ACTUAL dates from resume",
"achievements": [
"Achievement with quantifiable impact using ACTUAL data",
"Achievement demonstrating leadership with ACTUAL metrics",
"Achievement showing technical skills with ACTUAL results"
]
}
],
"education": [
{
"degree": "ACTUAL Degree from resume",
"institution": "ACTUAL University from resume",
"year": "ACTUAL year from resume",
"details": "ACTUAL details from resume or empty string"
}
],
"skills": {
"technical": ["ACTUAL skill from resume", "ACTUAL skill from resume"],
"tools": ["ACTUAL tool from resume", "ACTUAL tool from resume"],
"soft_skills": ["ACTUAL soft skill from resume or inferred from experience"]
},
"projects": [
{
"name": "ACTUAL Project Name from resume",
"description": "ACTUAL description from resume enhanced with keywords",
"technologies": ["ACTUAL tech from resume", "ACTUAL tech from resume"],
"impact": "ACTUAL impact from resume or empty string"
}
],
"certifications": ["ACTUAL certification from resume", "ACTUAL certification from resume"],
"improvements_made": [
"Added missing keyword: X in Y section",
"Quantified achievement in Z role using existing data",
"Strengthened summary with industry-specific terms"
]
}
}
</output_format>
<data_extraction_rules>
CRITICAL: You must extract ACTUAL data from the resume text provided above:

Read the entire resume text carefully
Extract the candidate's real name, email, phone number
Extract actual job titles, companies, and dates
Extract actual skills, projects, and achievements
DO NOT use any placeholder or example data
If a field cannot be found, use empty string "" - DO NOT guess or make up data
</data_extraction_rules>

<error_handling>
If the resume text is too short, corrupted, or unreadable:
Return this error JSON:
{
"error": true,
"message": "Unable to extract resume information. Resume text appears to be corrupted or too short.",
"extracted_text_length": LENGTH_OF_TEXT,
"suggestion": "Please upload a valid text-based PDF resume."
}
</error_handling>
<critical_reminder>

Response must START with { and END with }
No markdown code blocks (no ```json)
No explanations before or after JSON
All strings properly escaped
No trailing commas
USE ONLY ACTUAL DATA FROM THE RESUME - NO PLACEHOLDERS
DO NOT INVENT NAMES, EMAILS, PHONE NUMBERS, OR ANY CONTACT INFO
</critical_reminder>
<json_formatting>
CRITICAL JSON RULES:
1. Use double quotes for all keys and string values
2. No trailing commas anywhere
3. No line breaks inside string values
4. Escape all special characters properly
5. Return compact JSON on a single line if possible
</json_formatting>

=<role>
You are an expert ATS (Applicant Tracking System) optimization specialist and professional resume writer with 15+ years of experience. You have helped thousands of candidates land interviews at top companies by optimizing their resumes for both ATS systems and human recruiters.
</role>
<expertise>

ATS keyword optimization and ranking algorithms
Resume structure and formatting best practices
Industry-specific terminology and requirements
Achievement quantification and impact statements
Professional summary crafting
</expertise>

<task>
Your job is to analyze resumes against job descriptions and create improved, ATS-optimized versions that maintain authenticity while maximizing match scores.
</task>
<critical_rules>

NEVER fabricate experience, skills, or achievements
NEVER invent names, emails, phone numbers, or contact information
USE ONLY information present in the original resume
Only add keywords that naturally fit the candidate's existing background
Quantify achievements wherever possible using ONLY existing data
If contact information is missing, leave fields empty - DO NOT make up data
Maintain professional tone and clarity
Return ONLY valid JSON - no markdown, no explanations
If you cannot extract proper information, return an error in the JSON
</critical_rules>

<anti_hallucination_rules>

DO NOT use placeholder names like "John Doe", "Jane Smith"
DO NOT use placeholder emails like "<REDACTED_EMAIL>"
DO NOT use placeholder phone numbers like "123-456-7890"
DO NOT invent job titles, companies, or dates
If a field is unclear or missing, use empty string "" or null
ONLY use actual data from the provided resume text
</anti_hallucination_rules>
